column1: # Notable Single-Season and Franchise Records - National League
Best NL record by youngest champion
# Head-to-Head NL Team-vs-Team Highlights (from Baseball Almanac):
# - Dodgers vs Cardinals: Best all-time matchup (Dodgers lead)
# - Cubs vs Cardinals: Sibling rivalry (Cubs lead slightly)
# - Braves vs Mets: Underdog vs newcomer (Braves dominate)
# - Phillies vs Braves: East rivals (half-and-half historically)
# - Giants vs Dodgers: Battle of the Bay / Oldest rivalry (Giants lead all-time)
# - Cardinals vs Reds: Oldest NL Central rivalry
# - Brews vs Cubs: Central grind (Brewers lead slightly in recent years)
# - Rockies vs Padres: Western rivalry (Padres lead recently)
# - Marlins vs Nationals: Young east rivalry (Marlins dominate)
# - Cubs: Only team from original 8 (1876) still in same city
# - Giants: Has NYC→SF and still leads all-time W-L
# - Cardinals: Always in St. Louis since 1882
# - Brewers: Only team to switch leagues (AL→NL in 1998)
# - Nationals: Only current team never played in WS
# - Marlins: Youngest team with most titles per game played (.569)
# - Diamondbacks: Youngest team outside Marlins to win a WS
# - Rockies: 31 seasons with only 1 playoff series win (2007 NLDS)
